label: Onions, Garlic, Leeks, Chives, Scallions
effect: Destroys red blood cells → hemolytic anemia. Cats are 3–5× more sensitive than dogs.

label: Grapes & Raisins
effect: Acute kidney failure

label: Chocolate
effect: Cardiac, neurological, fatal

label: Xylitol / Birch Sugar
effect: Rapid hypoglycemia, liver failure. Severe in dogs; less severe but still avoid in cats.

label: Macadamia Nuts
effect: Weakness, tremors, hyperthermia. Worse in dogs but avoid in cats.

label: Avocado
effect: Vomiting, diarrhea; pit is choking + obstruction risk

label: Tomatoes (green/leaves)
effect: Tremors, GI issues — ripe red flesh usually OK

label: Raw Bread Dough
effect: Bloat, alcohol poisoning

label: Alcohol
effect: Same as humans but much smaller dose lethal

label: Caffeine
effect: Like chocolate — cardiac, neurological

label: Wild Mushrooms
effect: Unknown without ID — assume toxic

label: Cooked Bones (all)
effect: Choking, GI perforation, internal bleeding

label: Nutmeg
effect: Tremors, seizures

label: Raw Potato (green)
effect: GI issues

label: Rhubarb Leaves
effect: Kidney damage

label: Cherry Pits
effect: Toxic

label: Walnuts (especially moldy/black)
effect: Seizures

label: Salt (excess)
effect: Sodium ion poisoning — vomiting, tremors, seizures

label: Milk & Lactose Dairy
effect: Most adult dogs and cats are lactose intolerant — diarrhea, gas. The cat-milk-saucer image is a myth.

label: Raw Egg Whites (regular feeding)
effect: Long-term feeding binds biotin → skin/coat issues. Whole raw eggs OK occasionally; cooked is safer.

label: Hops (brewing)
effect: Malignant hyperthermia, fatal in some breeds

label: Peach, Plum, Apricot Pits
effect: Choking, intestinal blockage, cyanide poisoning

label: Yeast Dough (rising)
effect: Bloat (GDV risk), alcohol poisoning

label: Currants
effect: Acute kidney failure

label: Raw Freshwater Fish (staple)
effect: Destroys thiamine (B1) → neurological damage, seizures. Cooking inactivates the enzyme. Occasional small amounts of cooked freshwater fish are OK.

label: Tuna as a Staple
effect: Mercury accumulation; steatitis (yellow fat disease) from polyunsaturated fats without sufficient vitamin E. Cap at ≤ 10% of intake and rotate.

label: Liver in Excess
effect: Vitamin A toxicity (hypervitaminosis A) — bone deformation, joint disease. Cap liver at 5–10 g/day for a 4 kg cat. Cats are uniquely sensitive.

label: Dog Food Long-Term
toxicCompound: Missing taurine + arachidonic acid + preformed vitamin A
effect: Not acutely toxic, but lethally inadequate over months. Cats fed dog food develop dilated cardiomyopathy, retinal degeneration, and fatty liver.

label: Lilies (all parts, environment)
effect: Not a food, but household contact is enough — even pollen on fur, ingested while grooming, causes acute kidney failure within 36–72h. Remove all lilies (Lilium and Hemerocallis) from any home with a cat.

label: Plant-Only / Vegan Diet
effect: Cats lack the enzymes to derive arachidonic acid, taurine, vitamin A, vitamin D, and arginine from plant sources. Vegan cat diets are not safe at any duration.
